ConditionAssessment.description:
Output of the photo-pass vision call.

Per-room grades are nullable because the model is instructed to
only grade rooms it can clearly see. damp_signs and
structural_concerns are empty lists when nothing was flagged - the
consumer should treat empty as 'no evidence', not 'unknown'.

overall_confidence_pct = 0 with notes='vision parse failed' is the
canonical low-confidence sentinel returned when the JSON parse
layer fails (Phase 2.4 fallback path).

photo_assessments (v2, 2026-06-11) defaults to an empty list so
every v1 payload - Redis cache entries, persisted
properties.vision_condition_assessment JSONB, old fixtures - still
validates. Consumers treat an empty list as 'photo-level data not
available', not as 'no photos'.
ConditionAssessment.title: ConditionAssessment

bearerAuth.description: Paid API key issued from the dashboard. Send it as `Authorization: Bearer paid_...`.

info.description:
REST API for UK property data, market intelligence, investment analysis, and AI-powered search. Access 2.1M+ live and sold UK properties with PostGIS spatial search, BMV scoring, rental yields, HM Land Registry history, EPC certificates, planning + environmental constraints, and a multi-agent AI chat system.

**Authentication:** send your key as `Authorization: Bearer paid_...`. All endpoints require a paid subscription — there is no free tier. Responses are wrapped in a `{ data, meta }` envelope. See https://docs.propaideals.co.uk for guides, rate limits, and error codes.
info.title: Prop AI Deals API

Return ranked comparable sales near a postcode.

Adapts the unified comparables ranking core. Each comparable carries the
same confidence tier / transaction-category / flip signals the rest of
the platform uses.

Market demand / turnover for an outcode.

Compares active sale listings against properties marked sold/under-offer
in the last 90 days to derive a turnover ratio + a seller/buyer/balanced
rating, plus average days-on-market for live listings.

Return area demographics for a postcode district (e.g. M1, SW1, EH4).

Combines ONS census data, ONS deprivation index, and police.uk crime
statistics into one record per district. Updated quarterly.

Return EPC certificate records for properties in a postcode.

Joins `property_epc_data` to `properties` so the response includes the
property address. Returns up to `limit` records ordered by EPC date desc.
Return the EPC record for a specific Prop AI Deals property UUID.
Return the EPC record matching a specific UPRN, if any.

Check if a coordinate falls inside a UK flood risk zone.

Uses PostGIS ST_Contains against the flood_zones polygons (sourced from
SEPA, EA flood map, etc.). Returns the highest-risk match if a point
falls inside multiple zones.

Check flood risk for a Prop AI Deals property by UUID.

Looks up the property's lat/lon and runs the same PostGIS containment
check. Honours the property's pre-computed `is_flood_zone` and
`flood_zone` columns when available, falling back to the spatial join.

Year-on-year capital growth for an outcode from Land Registry medians.

Compares the median sold price of the last 12 months against the prior 12
months to compute a YoY growth percentage.

Aggregate listed buildings + conservation properties in an outcode.

Returns counts plus a small sample of each so consumers can spot-check.
Useful for due diligence on heritage-heavy areas where extension
permissions are restricted.

Return listed-building and conservation-area status for a property.

Uses the pre-computed `is_listed_building` and `in_conservation_area`
flags on the properties table. Both are populated by the planning
enrichment workers from Historic England + local authority data.

AI refurb estimate from user-uploaded photos (standalone calculator).

Pro/Founder only - consumes ONE of the user's monthly refurb-vision
allowance (15/month), the SAME counter as the property-page scan.
Images are sent inline to the model as base64 data URIs and are NOT
stored. HTTP 402 when the monthly quota is exhausted; 403 when the
tier has no scan allowance; 401 when not signed in.

get.description:
Get a smart refurbishment cost estimate for a property.

Uses property condition, size, type, and EPC data to produce
a data-driven estimate broken down by trade category.

When ``use_vision=true`` and the user is on Pro or Founder, the
service additionally runs a GPT-4o-mini photo pass against the
property's interior images (and a floorplan pass when
``floor_area_sqft`` is missing) to derive condition grades from
real evidence. Vision spend is bounded per user per month (premium 5,
pro/founder 50 - see REFURB_VISION_PER_MONTH) - a 30-day Redis cache on the assessment
means re-runs of the same property are free. HTTP 402 is returned
when the monthly quota is exhausted; HTTP 403 when the tier does
not include refurb-vision.

parameters.description: Enable AI photo+floorplan grading (per-plan monthly quota: premium 5, pro/founder 50). Vision-derived spec tiers and damp-structural force-select win over the manual condition_tier path.

get.description:
Per-council HMO landscape — Article 4 directions + licensing schemes.

Joins:
  * ``digital_land_article_4_directions WHERE is_hmo=true`` aggregated per
    ``council_name`` for directions count + geometry quality summary
  * ``hmo_licensing_schemes`` per council via ``local_authorities`` for the
    licensing scheme type tally (mandatory / additional / selective)

The two tables don't share an FK — A4 uses free-text ``council_name``,
licensing joins via ``local_authorities.name``. The join here is on
``LOWER(council_name) = LOWER(la.name)`` which catches the common forms
and degrades gracefully (zero on the licensing side, not a missing row).

Requires Premium plan or higher.

open markets in one table.

Batch property search endpoint for loading multiple pages in a single request.

This endpoint optimizes the common pattern of loading multiple pages
by executing a single database query and paginating results in-memory.
This reduces database load and improves response times by 6-10x.

Performance Benefits:
- 6 pages: 12 seconds -> 2 seconds (6x faster)
- Single database query instead of 6 separate queries
- Reduced connection pool pressure
- Lower latency from connection reuse

Request Body:
    search_request: Standard PropertySearchRequest with filters
    pages: List of page numbers to load (e.g., [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6])

Returns:
    Dictionary mapping page numbers to PropertySearchResponse objects
    Example: {1: {...}, 2: {...}, 3: {...}}

Limitations:
    - Maximum 10 pages per request to prevent abuse
    - Total results limited to 10 * per_page (default 200)

    db: Database session

get.description:

property metadata (if any). UPRN matching has multiple confidence tiers
— the response includes the source so clients can decide whether to
trust the match.
